step.title: Extract Findings and Return
step.goal: Synthesize observations from the run into a structured findings block that the caller (ticket-grill) can append to the bundle's research file. Return the block + the preserved prototype path. Do NOT write a separate findings document.

substep.title: Synthesize Findings Block
Update Prototype README with Final Status
substep.action:
Edit {{session.prototype_path}}/README.md to add a final section:
## Findings (captured)
Findings recorded in: {{session.bundle_dir}}/research-*-{{session.slug}}.md on {{ISO 8601 datetime}}.
See that file for the full finding block. This prototype is now evidence — preserved but not actively maintained.
Iterations: {{session.iteration_count + 1}} Status: {{answered | partially_answered}}
Use the Edit tool.

substep.title: Return to Caller
substep.action:
Compose the return payload:
{ status: "answered" | "partially_answered" | "aborted" | "escalate_to_spike", findings_block: {{session.findings_block}} (or null if aborted), prototype_path: {{session.prototype_path}}, prototype_name: {{session.prototype_name}}, branch: {{session.branch}}, iteration_count: {{session.iteration_count + 1}}, notes: {{any caveats or follow-ups for the caller}} }
Return this payload as the skill's final output. The caller (ticket-grill in step-05) is responsible for: - Appending findings_block to the bundle's research file - Logging the prototype invocation in the questions file - Stamping last_updated on the research file
ticket-prototype does NOT write to the research or questions files directly — that responsibility stays with the caller to keep the bundle's update path coherent in one place.

substep.title: Present Final Output

section.title: The findings absorption pattern
section.description:
Per QRDSPIV: "the answer extracted from a prototype belongs in the parent artifact". For ticketsmith, the "parent artifact" is the bundle's research file (kind: research). Prototype findings absorb into research findings; the prototype itself is preserved as a reference path linked from the finding.
There is NO separate prototype-findings document. This was a possible reading of spec §4.1's "Prototype doc(s)" listing, but Lock 38 supersedes that: findings absorb, prototypes preserve.

section.title: Why ticket-prototype doesn't write the research file directly
section.description:
The bundle's update path is owned by ticket-grill's step-05 (update-bundle-files). Centralizing all bundle writes in one place keeps the audit trail coherent — every bundle edit comes from one skill's responsibility. If ticket-prototype wrote directly to the research file, there would be two places that touch it, and bundle updates would diverge.
Return the findings_block; let the caller decide where and when to write it. This also lets the caller (or future grill-adopting agents) include the findings under their own appropriate research section structure.

section.title: When prototype findings are negative
section.description: "X does not work" is a valid finding. "X works only under condition Y" is a valid finding. "The prototype crashed in a way that revealed underlying constraint Z" is a valid finding. Negative evidence saves implementation time downstream.

rules.rule: Aborted prototypes still preserve the prototype_path so the caller can record what was attempted
The findings_block is null on abort, but the caller logs the attempt in the questions file
An aborted prototype is not a failure of the skill — it is information ("this question is not prototype-able")
